headline: East Malaysia Ports Fall 800,000 TEUs Short of Demand
context: Combined container handling at East Malaysian ports reached only 2.8 million TEUs in 2025 against demand of 3.6 million.

cards.big: East Malaysia ports handle only 2.8 million TEUs against 3.6 million demand
cards.sub: Capacity shortfall forces 22% of Borneo cargo through Peninsula ports adding cost

cards.big: KK port operates at 118% capacity handling 1.4 million TEUs annually
cards.sub: Average vessel waiting time increased from 2 days to 5 days since 2023

cards.big: Bintulu port expansion allocated RM2.8 billion but completion delayed to 2029
cards.sub: Original 2026 deadline missed due to contractor insolvency and redesign

cards.big: Sarawak proposed RM4 billion deepwater port at Tanjung Manis for Panamax vessels
cards.sub: Federal Transport Ministry has not approved project citing competing priorities

cards.big: Port shortfalls test whether trade infrastructure matches East Malaysia economic needs
800,000 TEUs short — East Malaysian ports need capacity investment before trade growth can materialise.
